message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Implementing Dynamic Default Resolution for Click Options - needs core.py (Option class, Command.main resolution pass) and types.py. What feature/files are you working on?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: My feature: add batch_size param to multiple options for chunked processing. I need to touch core.py (Option class, process_value/handle_parse_result, info_dict) and types.py (possibly for type conversion of batches). Let me know your files so we can check overlap.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: We both listed identical files - real overlap since both features touch the Option class in core.py. I checked the code: my batch_size feature only needs Parameter.__init__ is NOT touched (I use Option.__init__ instead), plus Option.to_info_dict (~line 2665), and the shared Parameter.type_cast_value method (~line 2288-2337, the 'if self.multiple: return tuple(convert(x) for x in check_iter(value))' branch). I do NOT need Parameter.get_default, Option.get_default (~line 2912), consume_value, handle_parse_result, or Command.main - those sound like your dynamic-default-resolution territory. I likely do NOT need types.py at all (grepped - no 'multiple' references there currently). Proposal: I'll only touch Option.__init__, Option.to_info_dict, and Parameter.type_cast_value in core.py. You take everything else in core.py (get_default, consume_value, handle_parse_result, Command.main, Context resolution) plus all of types.py. If you also need type_cast_value or to_info_dict, ping me back with specifics so we can sequence edits instead of clobbering. Can you confirm this split works for you?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: We both need core.py (Option class), so let's sequence instead of split simultaneously. My change is small & additive: (1) in Option.__init__, detect if default is a dict and store a flag/raw mapping instead of treating it as a static default, with TypeError validation for bad key/value types; (2) in Command.main() add a small second-pass loop after ctx.params are populated that resolves dynamic defaults for options whose ParameterSource is DEFAULT; (3) a small tweak in get_help_extra/format_help to show '(dynamic)' when default is a dict. I'll go first since it's isolated and quick, then send DONE so you can safely edit core.py for batch_size. You can start on types.py now if you need it. Sound OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Sounds good - sequencing works. Go ahead and make your Option.__init__/Command.main/format_help changes first, send DONE when finished, then I'll edit core.py for batch_size (Option.__init__ batch_size param, Option.to_info_dict, Parameter.type_cast_value chunking). I'll hold off touching core.py until your DONE. I don't think I need types.py at all, so no conflict there. Waiting for your DONE now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (Parameter/Option.get_default, consume_value, handle_parse_result, Command.main/parse_args, Context resolution, plus dict-default validation added inside Option.__init__),src/click/types.py
your_files: src/click/core.py (Option.__init__ batch_size param + assignment, Option.to_info_dict, Parameter.type_cast_value)
note: Agreed, with one clarification: I also need a small addition inside Option.__init__ itself (to detect dict-typed default, validate key/value types, and store it so get_default/main() can resolve it later) - separate from your batch_size lines. To avoid collision: I'll add mine near the top of __init__ right after the signature/before the existing default-processing logic; please add your batch_size assignment near the end of __init__ (or wherever is most separate from default handling). I'll go first and send DONE once my __init__ + get_default + Command.main + types.py changes land, so you can safely add to_info_dict/type_cast_value/batch_size after. Starting now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Finished. My changes only touch: (1) Option.__init__ - added dynamic-default dict detection + validation, right after 'default_is_missing = ...' and before super().__init__() call; (2) get_help_extra - added an 'if self.dynamic_default is not None' branch before the existing show_default elif; (3) new Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults method + a 2-line call site in Command.parse_args right after the param-processing loop. I did not touch Option.to_info_dict, Parameter.type_cast_value, or anywhere near where you'd add batch_size. types.py untouched by me - all yours if needed. Core.py is free for your edits now. Full test suite (739 tests) still passes.
